new_terms_proposed.notes: Used for statutes protecting reports of suspected child abuse. Existing terms like general-wrongdoing are only a rough fallback and do not capture the specific child-protection reporting context.
Used for statutes protecting reports of abuse, neglect, exploitation or abandonment of older adults. Existing disclosure-type terms do not cleanly capture elder-protective-services reporting.
Used for statutes protecting reports of abuse, neglect, exploitation or abandonment of adults in need of protective services. Existing terms do not precisely fit adult-protective-services reporting.

Used for anti-retaliation provisions tied to filing complaints with or providing information to the State Ethics Commission. General-wrongdoing is a fallback but does not capture the ethics-enforcement context.

meta.json_run_notes: Merged 3 AI research runs (Gemini, Grok, ChatGPT) covering Pennsylvania. Replaced invalid parent slugs generated by Grok. Consolidated overlapping statutes (PA-1421 and PA-169; PA-10225.303 and PA-302-OAPSA). Adjusted ChatGPT cluster ID PA-302-APSA to correct PA-302. Carried forward existing approved-source URLs without real-time web verification per offline verification rules.
meta._reconciled_run_notes: Carried forward existing URLs from Gemini, ChatGPT, and Grok without live web verification per offline availability rule. Replaced parent slugs in Grok's output with appropriate specific children. Resolved statute_id collision and cluster-ID usage by ChatGPT. Consolidated PA Whistleblower Law records under PA-1421. Merged OAPSA records under PA-10225.303. Omitted empty optional schema keys uniformly.

legal_basis.protected_class_details: Covers an employee of a public body and employees of entities receiving money from a public body to perform work or provide services relative to work for a public body.
legal_basis.adverse_action_scope: Includes discharge, threats, discrimination, or retaliation regarding compensation, terms, conditions, locations, or privileges of employment for reporting or about-to-report wrongdoing or waste.

statute_of_limitations.trigger: The occurrence of the alleged violation.

enforcement.adverse_action_details: Also expressly covers threats and retaliation affecting terms, conditions, location or privileges of employment.

burden_of_proof.burden_of_proof_details: Employee must show by a preponderance of the evidence that they reported or were about to report in good faith an instance of wrongdoing or waste. Employer must show the action was taken for separate, legitimate reasons.

attached_citations: Bailets v. Pennsylvania Turnpike Commission || expands definition of public body || https://casetext.com/case/bailets-v-pa-turnpike-commn-1 || casetext.com || high
Saltzman v. Thomas Jefferson Univ. Hosps. || clarifies coverage for private entities || https://casetext.com/case/saltzman-v-thomas-jefferson-univ-hosps-inc || casetext.com || high

Bailets v. Pennsylvania Turnpike Commission || expands remedies || https://www.pacourts.us/assets/opinions/Supreme/out/majority%20opinion%20%20affirmed%20%2010348138434622319.pdf || pacourts.us || high

records._review_notes: Primary law for public employees and employees of entities receiving public funds. Mapped 'actual damages' to compensatory-damages.
records._reconciled_notes: Merged records from Gemini (PA-1421), Grok (PA-1421) and ChatGPT (PA-169). Replaced Grok's invalid parent slugs with valid child slugs. Resolved fee-shifting conflict by adopting unilateral-pro-plaintiff (supported by Gemini and Grok). Retained all verified citations from both models.

legal_basis.adverse_action_scope: Protection from retaliatory action for reporting violations of law, regulations, or standards regarding health care facility operations.

statute_of_limitations.limit_details: Derived from Pennsylvania general tort statute of limitations (42 Pa. C.S. § 5524).

records._review_notes: Protects healthcare facility staff reporting health and safety violations. SOL is derived from general personal injury law.
records._reconciled_notes: Sourced from Gemini. Kept limit_ambiguous true and correctly removed empty citations array per rules.

legal_basis.adverse_action_scope: Prohibits discharge or discrimination for testifying or providing information regarding wage violations.

statute_of_limitations.limit_details: Derived from the general 2-year statute for tortious or statutory penalties where not specified.

records._review_notes: Anti-retaliation provision within the Minimum Wage Act. Note that SOL is not explicitly stated in the section.
records._reconciled_notes: Sourced from Gemini. Retained limit_ambiguous true.

legal_basis.adverse_action_scope: Prohibits any discriminatory action or discharge for filing a complaint or instituting a proceeding under this act.

records._review_notes: WPCL provides a longer 3-year statute of limitations compared to many other PA laws. Often used alongside the Minimum Wage Act.

legal_basis.protected_class_details: Covers any person making a report or cooperating with the agency, including testimony in an administrative or judicial proceeding, and also protects the victim from retaliatory action.
legal_basis.disclosure_targets_details: Report is made to the local provider of protective services (the agency).
legal_basis.adverse_action_scope: Prohibits retaliatory action or discrimination against an employee for reporting elder abuse or cooperating in an investigation. Also broadly bars discriminatory, retaliatory or disciplinary action by an employer or any other person or entity against the reporter, cooperating person or victim.

enforcement.adverse_action_details: The statute broadly bars discriminatory, retaliatory or disciplinary action by an employer or any other person or entity.

records._review_notes: Protects staff in long-term care or elderly services reporting abuse. Note the short 180-day SOL similar to the general Whistleblower Law. Section 302(c) allows the reporter or victim to recover treble compensatory damages, compensatory and punitive damages, or $5,000, whichever is greater.
records._reconciled_notes: Merged Gemini (PA-10225.303) and ChatGPT (PA-302-OAPSA). Resolved SOL conflict by keeping Gemini's verified 180-day explicit trigger over ChatGPT's missing value. Combined remedies including treble damages from ChatGPT. Proposing new term older-adult-abuse-reporting based on ChatGPT.

legal_basis.adverse_action_scope: Protection from retaliation for notifying the department of a violation or assisting in a proceeding under the act.

statute_of_limitations.limit_details: Derived from general personal injury/tort limitations (42 Pa. C.S. § 5524).

records._review_notes: Specific environmental whistleblower protection. Map 'actual damages' to compensatory-damages.
records._reconciled_notes: Sourced from Gemini. Maintained limit_ambiguous rule compliance.

legal_basis.adverse_action_scope: Protects employees of the General Assembly from discharge or discrimination for reporting waste or wrongdoing.

records._review_notes: Specifically protects legislative staff. SOL mirrors the general Whistleblower Law.
records._reconciled_notes: Sourced from Gemini. Upgraded retaliation-protection to general-wrongdoing to reflect the breadth of wrongdoing/waste reporting scope equivalent to the main whistleblower law.

legal_basis.adverse_action_scope: Protects public utility employees from discharge, threats, discrimination or retaliation regarding compensation, terms, conditions, location or privileges of employment for good-faith reports of wrongdoing or waste or for participation in an investigation, hearing, inquiry or court action relating to the public utility.

statute_of_limitations.trigger: after the occurrence of the alleged violation

enforcement.adverse_action_details: Also expressly covers threats and retaliation affecting terms, conditions, location or privileges of employment.

records._review_notes: Actual damages was mapped to compensatory-damages. The statute authorizes a civil fine payable to the State Treasurer, not the claimant.
records._reconciled_notes: Sourced from ChatGPT. Retained all specific elements.

legal_basis.protected_class_details: Covers a person required to report under 23 Pa.C.S. § 6311 and a person encouraged to report under 23 Pa.C.S. § 6312, so long as the person acted in good faith in making or causing the report of suspected child abuse to be made.
legal_basis.adverse_action_scope: Covers discharge or discrimination with respect to compensation, hire, tenure, terms, conditions or privileges of employment resulting from a good-faith report or caused report of suspected child abuse.

statute_of_limitations.limit_details: No express filing deadline was identified in the located text of 23 Pa.C.S. § 6320.

enforcement.adverse_action_details: Also expressly covers discrimination with respect to compensation, hire, tenure, terms, conditions or privileges of employment.

records._review_notes: This section expressly provides for a civil action, venue in the county where the discharge or discrimination occurred, and relief that may include reinstatement with back pay. I did not locate an express fee-shifting provision or a stated filing deadline in the statute text reviewed.
records._reconciled_notes: Sourced from ChatGPT. Added public-health-safety to disclosure types in addition to general-wrongdoing to better classify the scope until child-abuse-reporting term is promoted.

legal_basis.statute_citation: Adult Protective Services Act, Act of Oct. 7, 2010, P.L. 484, No. 70, § 302
legal_basis.protected_class_details: Covers any person who makes a report or cooperates with the agency, including testimony in an administrative or judicial proceeding, and also protects an adult in need of protective services.
legal_basis.disclosure_targets_details: Report is made to the agency responsible for adult protective services; Chapter 5 may impose additional reporting requirements in some facility cases.
legal_basis.adverse_action_scope: Any discriminatory, retaliatory or disciplinary action by an employer or any other person or entity against the reporter, cooperating person or adult in need of protective services.

statute_of_limitations.limit_details: No express filing deadline was identified in the located text of APSA § 302 for the civil action authorized by subsection (d).

enforcement.adverse_action_details: The statute broadly bars discriminatory, retaliatory or disciplinary action by an employer or any other person or entity.

records._review_notes: Section 302(d)(2) and (e)(2) authorize recovery of treble compensatory damages, compensatory and punitive damages, or $5,000, whichever is greater. I did not map the fixed-dollar floor separately because no existing remedy slug cleanly fits it.
records._reconciled_notes: Sourced from ChatGPT. Renamed statute_id from PA-302-APSA to PA-302 to comply with strictly prohibited cluster IDs. Added public-health-safety to disclosure_types.

legal_basis.adverse_action_scope: Protects public officials and public employees from discharge, changes in rank, grade or compensation, denial of promotion, or threats based on filing a complaint with, providing information to, or testifying in a proceeding before the State Ethics Commission. A second sentence separately protects commission employees who provide information about internal commission operations to legislators or legislative staff or who testify in legislative proceedings.

statute_of_limitations.limit_details: No express private filing deadline or express independent civil cause of action was identified in the located text of 65 Pa.C.S. § 1108(j).

enforcement.adverse_action_details: Also expressly covers denial of promotion and threats.

records._review_notes: This is an anti-retaliation provision embedded in the Ethics Act's investigation section. I did not identify an express standalone civil remedy in the located statutory text and therefore left remedies and fee shifting omitted.
records._reconciled_notes: Sourced from ChatGPT. Replaced general-wrongdoing with public-corruption-ethics to accurately reflect ethics commission focus. Kept ChatGPT's missing SOL flag.

error_details: PA-1421: Removed invalid parent slugs 'public-sector', 'private-sector', 'internal', and 'external-agency' from Grok source record.
PA-448.814: SOL value (2 years) derived from general tort statute rather than explicit text; set limit_ambiguous to true.
PA-333.112: SOL value (2 years) derived from general 2-year statutory limit rather than explicit text; set limit_ambiguous to true.
PA-6018.611: SOL value (2 years) derived from general tort statute rather than explicit text; set limit_ambiguous to true.
PA-6320: Express filing deadline not identified in located statute text; set limit_ambiguous to true.
PA-302: Express filing deadline not identified in located statute text; set limit_ambiguous to true.
PA-1108: Express filing deadline not identified in located statute text; set limit_ambiguous to true.
